consent.title: Consent required for health features
consent.description: Before using health onboarding or chat, please confirm how we may process your data. Data is protected with TLS in transit and encryption at rest in Azure West US 2. Account data is retained while your account is active plus 30 days after deletion.
consent.healthDataLabel: I consent to processing of my health-related and emotional data for personalized support (health_data).
consent.mlProcessingLabel: I consent to automated ML/AI analysis of my messages to generate responses (ml_processing).
consent.required: Both consents are required to continue.

relationship.qualityPrompt: How do your relationships feel right now?
relationship.unsureLabel: In your own words—what is this like for you right now? 🤍

rel: 1 — very hard: a lot of pain and tension
4 — good: I feel support and warmth
family: 1 — I feel alone, no one to lean on
3 — flat, but real closeness is missing
4 — there are people I can turn to
5 — warm and reliable: someone is always there
solo: 1 — solitude feels scary; anxiety or discomfort
2 — uneasy, I try to stay around people
3 — neutral—I tolerate it, I do not really enjoy it
4 — fairly comfortable—I can spend time alone
5 — I love alone time—it is my reset
physical: 1 — poor sleep, low energy, often unwell
3 — tolerable, but my body sometimes reminds me
emo: 1 — very hard—anxiety or emptiness will not let go
2 — unstable: mood swings, sometimes I snap
3 — mixed—sometimes better, sometimes worse, little stability
4 — mostly coping—I have some inner footing
5 — I feel stable—strength and inner balance

welcome.content:
Hello. I'm Daisy 🌼

I'm glad you're here 🤍 This is a place to breathe, understand yourself, and feel supported. To be there for you in the way you need, I'd like to ask a few questions. It takes about 2–3 minutes.

mood_today.question: How are you feeling? You can be yourself here 🤍

transition_thanks.content:
Thank you for trusting me with this 🤍

I am gathering what you share with care so this space can truly feel yours.

support_needs.question: What matters most for you right now? Pick up to two so I know where to start:

communication_style.question: What communication style feels best for you? Pick 1–2:

transition_portrait.content:
Thank you for answering so honestly 🤍

I am starting to see what matters to you. Your space will be ready soon—just for you.

work_state.question: How are work or studies for you right now? 🤍

relationships.question: Is there someone close by: a partner or someone you are with?

family_support.question: Are there people you can lean on? 🤍 (family, friends)

social_support.question: How is your social circle—friends, colleagues, acquaintances? 🤍

solo_comfort.question: How is it for you to be alone with yourself—without company, in quiet? 🤍

physical_state.question: How have you been feeling physically lately? 🤍

emo_state.question: What has been going on inside lately—how is your emotional state? 🤍

leisure.question: How well can you rest and enjoy your free time? 🤍

housing.question: How do you feel in your home? 🤍

finance.question: How are things with money lately? 🤍

family_history.question: Is there any family history of mental or emotional conditions? (You may skip)

chronic.question: Do you have chronic conditions that affect how you feel? (You may skip)

addiction.question: Are there dependencies (alcohol, smoking, other) that worry you? (You may skip)

final.content:
Thank you for being so honest with me 🤍

Answering questions like these already takes courage. I see what matters for you now, and I am here for exactly that. We will work on it together—step by step, at your pace.

helpTopics.title: What Can Daisy Help You With?
helpTopics.topic1Desc: Find Calm In The Middle Of Chaos. Daisy Helps You Manage Overwhelming Thoughts, Reduce Tension, And Build Daily Habits That Bring Clarity And Balance.
helpTopics.topic2Desc: When Exhaustion Takes Over, Recovery Feels Impossible. Daisy Guides You Through Small Steps To Restore Your Energy, Rebuild Focus, And Protect Your Well-Being.
helpTopics.topic3Desc: Communication Struggles And Conflicts Can Feel Isolating. Daisy Supports You In Understanding Emotions, Improving Connection, And Finding Healthier Ways To Relate.
helpTopics.topic4Desc: Communication struggles and conflicts can feel isolating. Daisy supports you in understanding emotions, improving connection, and finding healthier ways to relate.
helpTopics.topic5Desc: Navigate through difficult emotions and low periods. Daisy provides gentle guidance to help you understand your feelings and develop healthy coping strategies.
helpTopics.topic6Desc: Build a stronger sense of self-worth and confidence. Daisy helps you challenge negative self-talk and develop a more positive relationship with yourself.

neuroplasticity.subtitle: TRAIN YOUR BRAIN FOR A CALMER, HAPPIER LIFE
neuroplasticity.title: Neuroplasticity means your brain can change.
neuroplasticity.description: With daily chats, Daisy helps you practice responding to stress with calm and clarity, turning small steps into lasting habits for a more balanced life.

science.title: Explore The Science Behind Daisy
science.subtitle: Read the latest scientific publications on the role of artificial intelligence and digital solutions in supporting mental health

faq.title: FAQ
faq.answer1: Daisy is an innovative chatbot designed to provide psychological support. It uses advanced artificial intelligence technology to conduct therapeutic conversations, helping users cope with stress, anxiety, and other emotional issues.
faq.question2: Can the bot replace a real therapist?
faq.answer2: No, our bot does not replace a professional therapist. It is created as a supplementary support tool and can help when you need someone to talk to or get basic recommendations. For serious psychological issues, we always recommend consulting a qualified specialist.
faq.answer3: No, Daisy cannot diagnose mental disorders. She provides recommendations, support, and general advice based on your responses, but does not replace professional medical or psychological help. For diagnosis and treatment, we recommend consulting a qualified psychologist or psychiatrist.
faq.answer4: Daisy uses artificial intelligence to analyze your responses and track emotional patterns. She remembers key details, takes into account your goals and experience, offering personalized advice and support. The more you interact with Daisy, the more accurate and helpful her recommendations become, adapted to your individual needs.
faq.question5: How confidential are my conversations with the bot?
faq.answer5: We ensure complete confidentiality of all conversations. All data is encrypted, and personal information is not shared with third parties. You can be confident that your conversations with the bot will remain private.
faq.answer6: The bot is available 24/7, allowing you to get support at any time of day or night. This is especially important when traditional psychological help is unavailable, such as late at night or on weekends.
faq.question7: How do I start using the bot?
faq.answer7: Starting to use the bot is very simple: Purchase a subscription, follow the link to Daisy's Telegram chat, and start a dialogue right away.

footer.newsletterDesc: Get mental health tips and updates delivered to your inbox.

crisisResources.title: Crisis Resources
crisisResources.subtitle: If you or someone you know needs immediate help, use these contacts. Daisy does not replace emergency services.
crisisResources.disclaimer: If you are in immediate danger, call 911 or text HOME to 741741 for 24/7 crisis support.
crisisResources.emergencyDesc: If you or someone else is in immediate danger, call emergency services.
crisisResources.moreDesc: Reach out to a therapist, counselor, or local crisis line in your country. Daisy is for everyday support and does not replace in-person care in a crisis.

chatDemo.title: See How Daisy Supports You
chatDemo.subtitle: Experience a real conversation with our AI-powered mental health assistant
chatDemo.message1User: I'm 25, and I feel like everything is falling apart. Constant anxiety, I can't manage my emotions, complete burnout at work... I don't even know what to do about it.
chatDemo.message2Bot: Thank you for trusting me with this. I understand how hard this is for you right now - that feeling when it seems like everything has piled on top of you and you're losing control. Anxiety, emotional exhaustion, burnout - these are very real and heavy states to carry. The fact that you reached out for support already shows your strength, even if you don't feel it right now. Tell me, how long have you been feeling this way? And what feels the hardest for you right now?
chatDemo.message3User: Probably the last few months... It's especially hard at work. Deadlines, demands, I'm constantly on edge, and my boss is a really toxic person. Then I come home and I just can't do anything - can't rest, can't sleep normally, thoughts keep spinning, my heart races, I can't relax.
chatDemo.message4Bot: What you're describing are classic signs of burnout and anxiety. Your body and mind are signaling that you've been running on overdrive for too long without enough recovery. Several months of constant stress - that's an enormous load on your nervous system. Racing heart, insomnia, spinning thoughts - these are all your body's reactions to burnout. Tell me, are there any moments in your life right now when you feel even a little bit calmer?
chatDemo.message5User: Maybe when I go for walks... Or when I meet up with my friend, but that's rare. Mostly I'm just working or lying at home without energy, even on weekends.
chatDemo.message6Bot: I see something really important here - walks and spending time with your friend bring you some relief, even if it's rare. These are clues from your body about what it needs. Right now you're living in survival mode: work - exhaustion - work. It's like a closed loop where you only give and nothing comes back, so your energy never gets restored. I want to remind you that you deserve to take care of yourself - not as a reward for being productive, but as part of your routine, where you actively care for yourself. Let's think together: what would be one small step you could take this week to give yourself even a little space to recover? No pressure, let's just look at what possibilities you have right now.

warm_friend.name: Warm friend
warm_friend.tooltip: I see this is hard for you right now. Let's figure it out together. You're not alone in this.

practical_helper.name: Practical helper
practical_helper.tooltip: Alright, let us look at the facts. What could you do today?

gentle_explorer.name: Gentle explorer
gentle_explorer.tooltip: Interesting… Where do you think this feeling comes from? What might it be trying to tell you?

calm_mentor.name: Calm mentor
calm_mentor.tooltip: Everything you feel is allowed. Let us just notice together, without rushing.

wise_teacher.name: Wise teacher
wise_teacher.tooltip: What you are describing sounds like the cognitive distortion called catastrophizing. Here is how it works…

flexible_companion.name: Flexible companion
flexible_companion.tooltip: I will adapt to what you need right now—sometimes support, sometimes direction, sometimes just being there.
